title: Phyllanthus albus
intro: Phyllanthus albus, also known as white Phyllanthus or white gale of the desert, is a plant species native to various regions around the world, including Africa and Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and hepatoprotective properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating liver and kidney disorders, as well as other conditions such as diabetes and fever. The bioactive constituents of Phyllanthus albus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Phyllanthus albus is typically used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and supplements, allowing for its bioactive compounds to be harnessed for potential therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Phyllanthus albus is classified under the family Phyllanthaceae. This family is a part of the order Malpighiales. The order Malpighiales is further classified under the clade Rosids. The clade Rosids is a subclade of the clade Eurosids. Further classification includes the division Magnoliophyta, and the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Phyllanthus albus, a plant species native to tropical regions, has been found to possess various medicinal properties. It is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential in modern medicine. The plant's medicinal uses are still largely based on traditional knowledge and have not been extensively studied scientifically.

side_effects: Phyllanthus albus is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, but its use can cause side effects in some individuals. Phyllanthus albus may cause gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea in some people. Other potential side effects include allergic reactions, skin irritation, and interactions with certain medications.
